# Part 8 — Procedure for Miscellaneous Applications

## PART 8 E+W PROCEDURE FOR MISCELLANEOUS APPLICATIONS


### CHAPTER 1 E+W PROCEDURE


### Procedure E+W


8.1. Subject to rules 8.13 and8.24, applications to which this Part applies must be made in accordance with the Part 19 procedure.


### CHAPTER 2 E+W APPLICATION FOR CORRECTED GENDER RECOGNITION CERTIFICATE


### Scope of this Chapter E+W


8.2. The rules in this Chapter apply to an application under section 6(1) of the Gender Recognition Act 2004 for the correction of a full gender recognition certificate issued under section 5(1) or 5A(1) of that Act M1 .


Marginal Citations


M1 Section 5A(1) was inserted by section 250(1) and (4) of the Civil Partnership Act 2004.


### Where to start proceedings E+W


8.3. The application must be made to the court which issued the original certificate unless the court directs otherwise.


### Who the parties are E+W


8.4. Where the applicant is—


### Delivery of copy certificate to Secretary of State E+W


8.5. Where the court issues a corrected full gender recognition certificate, a court officer must send a copy of the corrected certificate to the Secretary of State.


### CHAPTER 3 E+W APPLICATION FOR ALTERATION OF MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT AFTER DEATH OF ONE PARTY


### Scope of this Chapter E+W


8.6. The rules in this Chapter apply to an application under section 36 of the 1973 Act M2 or paragraph 73 of Schedule 5 to the 2004 Act to alter a maintenance agreement after the death of one of the parties.


Marginal Citations


M2 Section 36 was amended by section 26(1) of the Inheritance (Provision for Family and Dependants) Act 1975 (c.63) .


### Where to start proceedings E+W


F1 8.7. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
